item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Surprising that this scip doesnt have more votes. Its unique, creative, and well executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simultaneously both fun and horrifying; a neat little skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding that the interview's a bit blatantly expositionary, but I absolutely adore the imagery, and I find it commendable that you've managed to describe it in a way that's both clear and evocative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fine by me. I look forward to seeing your take on the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No sweat mate, it hasn't been even posted yet. I like your idea so if you agree I'll just link your article when I do mine, researchers should notice and take note of the similarities when they occur. I'll also credit you as the person who fleshed out the idea before I did :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn son, and I thought I had scoured the site for anything like this; I never thought to check the INT branches. Didn't mean to swipe the idea out from under you, and don't feel the need to modify your ideas for my sake!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, considering that this is basically the essence of my idea that I've had for my Temporal Division and MTF canon the polish branch, all I can do is give it an upvote and modify my plans.

My original idea tho was about a cult of time followers who could gain minor Temporal shifting by simply accelerating or decreasing their speed of movement, but sometimes it glitches and they would end up like this guy here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this is why you don't run you time machine on windows 98. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The way you let the guy think containment's a temporary measure is an interesting and novel approach, and the anomaly itself is a perfect combination of mind-bending and cool. It's not perfect, but it's well-worth an upvote.

item.title: Author post?
item.content:encoded: I appreciate all the feedback, folks. I've trimmed a good deal from the interview and disposed of the addendum entirely. I know that the mainlist isn't supposed to be for drafting and critiques, so this'll be my last significant rewrite of this article. If it still bombs, I'll let it be. I'd love to see this idea fleshed out by someone else with a more skilled hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is OK, but as mentioned by others theres a lot of exposition here, and the characterisation of the SCP in the interview log is all over the place. Is he supposed to be an idiot or a genius? I can't tell because he seems to flit back and forth between the two constantly. Very jarring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not the best written, but really cool concept. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

While there's a LOT of exposition here, I still like it. I'm a sucker for time travel and 4th dimensional consciousness stuff, so kudos for creating a really interesting anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems mostly fine, but that interview is REALLY long-winded, and kind of repetitive to boot.

Also the addendum is sort of extraneous- feels like this might be better off as a 'less is more' situation.
